# @ezcorp/ai-kit

Integration kit for driving the EZCorp backend from any LLM (external harness or in-app agent).

## Start here

- [docs/OVERVIEW.md](docs/OVERVIEW.md): Conceptual model — projects, conversations, mentions, runs, agents, teams, tasks.
- [docs/quickstart-curl.md](docs/quickstart-curl.md): End-to-end recipe (auth → create conv → send message → stream).
- [docs/openapi.yaml](docs/openapi.yaml): Machine-readable REST spec.
- [docs/mentions.md](docs/mentions.md): Normative mention grammar for `!`, `@`, `/` sigils.
- [docs/events.md](docs/events.md): SSE run event taxonomy.

## Optional: installable surfaces

- MCP server: `bunx @ezcorp/ai-kit mcp` (stdio) or mount `/api/mcp` (Streamable HTTP).
- Claude Code skills: `bunx @ezcorp/ai-kit install claude-code`.
- EZCorp extension: `bunx @ezcorp/ai-kit install ezcorp`.

## Auth

Session cookie (`ezcorp_session`, 30-day JWT) OR `Authorization: Bearer ez_<key>` with scope `read` / `chat` / `admin`.

## Entity links

Tool responses for entity-producing tools include a `url` and `markdownLink` alongside the raw IDs — click the link in chat to jump to the new conversation, agent, sub-conversation, or run. URLs use `EZCORP_PUBLIC_URL` (or the `publicUrl` client option), falling back to `EZCORP_BASE_URL`. Set `EZCORP_PUBLIC_URL` on the server when the user-facing origin differs from the API origin (e.g. loopback API + public HTTPS domain).
